texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this project is to release a new national reminder and reminder dialog that will be used by Polytrauma Specialty providers to identify potential OEF/OIF Polytrauma patients and mark the patient with a Polytrauma Marker (health factor) when appropriate.
texts.text: The purpose of this project is to release a new national reminder and reminder dialog that will be used by Polytrauma Specialty providers to identify potential OEF/OIF Polytrauma patients and mark the patient with a Polytrauma Marker (health factor) when appropriate.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Polytrauma Marker project includes two patches:
texts.text: The Polytrauma Marker project includes two patches:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PXRM*2*17 -	Clinical Reminder patch with Polytrauma Marker reminder/dialog
texts.text: PXRM*2*17 -	Clinical Reminder patch with Polytrauma Marker reminder/dialog

texts.label: text
texts.orig: USR*1*33 -	ASU patch with API that checks to see if a user is a member of a specific User Class
texts.text: USR*1*33 -	ASU patch with API that checks to see if a user is a member of a specific User Class

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Clinical Reminders PXRM*2*17 and USR*1*33 Documentation
texts.text: Clinical Reminders PXRM*2*17 and USR*1*33 Documentation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: : In this document you will see references to both PXRM*2*17 and PXRM*2.0*17.  The difference is that PXRM*2*17 is the name of the patch and PXRM*2.0*17 is the name of the build.
texts.text: : In this document you will see references to both PXRM*2*17 and PXRM*2.0*17.  The difference is that PXRM*2*17 is the name of the patch and PXRM*2.0*17 is the name of the build.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This manual describes how to install the bundled patches, PXRM*2*17 and USR*1*33.
texts.text: This manual describes how to install the bundled patches, PXRM*2*17 and USR*1*33.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This build can be installed with users on the system, but it should be done during non-peak hours.
texts.text: This build can be installed with users on the system, but it should be done during non-peak hours.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The installation needs to be done by a person with DUZ(0) set to "@."
texts.text: The installation needs to be done by a person with DUZ(0) set to "@."

texts.orig: 1 Retrieve host file containing the multi-package build
texts.text: 1 Retrieve host file containing the multi-package build

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Use ftp to access the build (the name of the host file is POYLTRAUMA_MARKER.KID) from one of the following locations:
texts.text: Use ftp to access the build (the name of the host file is POYLTRAUMA_MARKER.KID) from one of the following locations:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2 Install the build first in a training or test account.
texts.text: 2 Install the build first in a training or test account.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Installing in a non-production environment will give you time to get familiar with new functionality and complete the setup for reminders and dialogs prior to installing the software in production.
texts.text: Installing in a non-production environment will give you time to get familiar with new functionality and complete the setup for reminders and dialogs prior to installing the software in production.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In programmer mode type, D ^XUP, select the Kernel Installation & Distribution System menu (XPD MAIN), then the Installation option, and then the option LOAD a Distribution.  Enter your directory name and POLYTRAUMA_MARKER.KID at the Host File prompt.
texts.text: In programmer mode type, D ^XUP, select the Kernel Installation & Distribution System menu (XPD MAIN), then the Installation option, and then the option LOAD a Distribution.  Enter your directory name and POLYTRAUMA_MARKER.KID at the Host File prompt.

texts.orig: This option will allow you to view all changes that will be made when the patch is installed.  It compares all components of the patch (routines, DDs, templates, etc.).
texts.text: This option will allow you to view all changes that will be made when the patch is installed.  It compares all components of the patch (routines, DDs, templates, etc.).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global.  If there are any discrepancies, do not run the Install Package(s) option. Instead, run the Unload a Distribution option to remove the Transport Global from your system.  Retrieve the file again from the anonymous directory (in case there was corruption in FTPing) and Load the Distribution again.  If the problem still exists, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the national Help Desk (1-888-596-HELP) to report the problem.
texts.text: This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global.  If there are any discrepancies, do not run the Install Package(s) option. Instead, run the Unload a Distribution option to remove the Transport Global from your system.  Retrieve the file again from the anonymous directory (in case there was corruption in FTPing) and Load the Distribution again.  If the problem still exists, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the national Help Desk (1-888-596-HELP) to report the problem.

Select the build POLYTRAUMA MARKER 1.0 and proceed with the install.  If you have problems with the installation, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the National Help Desk to report the problem.

Select the build POLYTRAUMA MARKER 1.0 and proceed with the install.  If you have problems with the installation, log a Remedy ticket and/or call the National Help Desk to report the problem.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This project was requested by the Office of Patient Care Services (PCS) to provide a means of improving care provided to veterans and active duty patients suffering from blast-related polytrauma (multiple complex injuries). VHA has identified this as one of several initiatives that will support health care of Operation Iraqi Freedom and Operation Enduring Freedom (OIF/OEF) veterans.
texts.text: This project was requested by the Office of Patient Care Services (PCS) to provide a means of improving care provided to veterans and active duty patients suffering from blast-related polytrauma (multiple complex injuries). VHA has identified this as one of several initiatives that will support health care of Operation Iraqi Freedom and Operation Enduring Freedom (OIF/OEF) veterans.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In order to provide the necessary specialized support and care for veterans with blast-related polytrauma injuries, it is essential to provide a means of easily identifying these veterans in the electronic health care record.  Clinicians need a way to quickly identify veterans with polytrauma injuries and Congress and Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) requires a means of tracking and reporting the care provided to these veterans.
texts.text: In order to provide the necessary specialized support and care for veterans with blast-related polytrauma injuries, it is essential to provide a means of easily identifying these veterans in the electronic health care record.  Clinicians need a way to quickly identify veterans with polytrauma injuries and Congress and Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) requires a means of tracking and reporting the care provided to these veterans.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This project includes new functionality that limits access of the reminder on the cover sheet to providers that are members of a locally defined Polytrauma rehabilitation user class.  The provider who is a member of this user class will be able to respond to the Polytrauma Marker reminder from the CPRS notes tab using a reminder dialog.
texts.text: This project includes new functionality that limits access of the reminder on the cover sheet to providers that are members of a locally defined Polytrauma rehabilitation user class.  The provider who is a member of this user class will be able to respond to the Polytrauma Marker reminder from the CPRS notes tab using a reminder dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sites currently use the Authorization/Subscription Utility software to control the level of authorization needed for signing specific document types and orderables. This will expand the use of the user class for reminder functionality.
texts.text: Sites currently use the Authorization/Subscription Utility software to control the level of authorization needed for signing specific document types and orderables. This will expand the use of the user class for reminder functionality.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A new national reminder computed finding, VA-ASU USER CLASS, has been created that can be used in reminder definitions, reminder terms, and reminder dialogs. This computed finding includes the ability to specify a user class showing that the user is active.
texts.text: A new national reminder computed finding, VA-ASU USER CLASS, has been created that can be used in reminder definitions, reminder terms, and reminder dialogs. This computed finding includes the ability to specify a user class showing that the user is active.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This computed finding can return multiple occurrences of a specified ASU user class for the user who is running the reminder evaluation or processing a reminder dialog.
texts.text: This computed finding can return multiple occurrences of a specified ASU user class for the user who is running the reminder evaluation or processing a reminder dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a user is assigned to an ASU User Class, there can be an Activation Date and an Expiration Date. For the purpose of this computed finding, the date of the finding will be the Activation Date. In those cases when there is no Activation Date, 00/00/0000 will be used.
texts.text: When a user is assigned to an ASU User Class, there can be an Activation Date and an Expiration Date. For the purpose of this computed finding, the date of the finding will be the Activation Date. In those cases when there is no Activation Date, 00/00/0000 will be used.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a Beginning Date/Time and Ending Date/Time are used with the finding, then the period defined by the Activation Date and Expiration Date must overlap with the period defined by the Beginning Date/Time and the Ending Date/Time in order for the finding to be true. When the Activation Date is missing, a 0 will be used in its place for determining the overlap. When the Expiration Date is missing, the evaluation date will be used in its place for determining the overlap.
texts.text: If a Beginning Date/Time and Ending Date/Time are used with the finding, then the period defined by the Activation Date and Expiration Date must overlap with the period defined by the Beginning Date/Time and the Ending Date/Time in order for the finding to be true. When the Activation Date is missing, a 0 will be used in its place for determining the overlap. When the Expiration Date is missing, the evaluation date will be used in its place for determining the overlap.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Computed Finding Parameter can be used to optionally specify either the internal entry number or the exact name of an ASU User Class. If this option is used, then each User Class the user is a member of is checked to see if it is either the specified User Class or a child of the specified User Class. Only the User Classes that pass this test will remain on the list of a user's User Classes. For example, if the user is a member of the user class ATTENDING PHYSICIAN which is a child of the user class PHYSICIAN and the Computed Finding Parameter is set to "PHYSICIAN" then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN would be true. If the Computed Finding Parameter was set to CARDIOLOGIST, then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN would be false since it is not a child of CARDIOLOGIST.
texts.text: The Computed Finding Parameter can be used to optionally specify either the internal entry number or the exact name of an ASU User Class. If this option is used, then each User Class the user is a member of is checked to see if it is either the specified User Class or a child of the specified User Class. Only the User Classes that pass this test will remain on the list of a user's User Classes. For example, if the user is a member of the user class ATTENDING PHYSICIAN which is a child of the user class PHYSICIAN and the Computed Finding Parameter is set to "PHYSICIAN" then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN would be true. If the Computed Finding Parameter was set to CARDIOLOGIST, then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN would be false since it is not a child of CARDIOLOGIST.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Polytrauma Assessment reminder definition contains a new national reminder term which will be pre-defined with the new computed finding for the ASU User Class.  Sites will be required to add the Computed Finding Parameter to the national reminder term.  The parameter should specify the local ASU User Class that identifies specialty provider members that focus on Polytrauma and Rehabilitation services at the local facility.
texts.text: The Polytrauma Assessment reminder definition contains a new national reminder term which will be pre-defined with the new computed finding for the ASU User Class.  Sites will be required to add the Computed Finding Parameter to the national reminder term.  The parameter should specify the local ASU User Class that identifies specialty provider members that focus on Polytrauma and Rehabilitation services at the local facility.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Polytrauma Assessment reminder uses a series of national Reminder Taxonomies to determine whether the patient has multiple diagnoses that identify the patient as a potential Polytrauma patient.  The combination of the diagnoses found and the user’s ASU user class will determine whether the reminder is applicable to the patient.
texts.text: The Polytrauma Assessment reminder uses a series of national Reminder Taxonomies to determine whether the patient has multiple diagnoses that identify the patient as a potential Polytrauma patient.  The combination of the diagnoses found and the user’s ASU user class will determine whether the reminder is applicable to the patient.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the reminder is applicable to the patient and the patient has not been previously assessed for Polytrauma, the reminder will be DUE and will appear on the cover sheet and in the reminder drawer on the CPRS notes tab.
texts.text: If the reminder is applicable to the patient and the patient has not been previously assessed for Polytrauma, the reminder will be DUE and will appear on the cover sheet and in the reminder drawer on the CPRS notes tab.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The reminder will be resolved by the provider responding to reminder dialog responses that result in the assessment that the patient “is” or “is not” an OEF/OIF Polytrauma patient.  The responses will cause Health Factors to be created that make the reminder no longer due.
texts.text: The reminder will be resolved by the provider responding to reminder dialog responses that result in the assessment that the patient “is” or “is not” an OEF/OIF Polytrauma patient.  The responses will cause Health Factors to be created that make the reminder no longer due.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If the CPRS user views Clinical Maintenance output for the Polytrauma Assessment reminder and the user is not a member of the ASU User Class, then a message will be included in the Patient Cohort section indicating that the ASU User Class was not found.
texts.text: If the CPRS user views Clinical Maintenance output for the Polytrauma Assessment reminder and the user is not a member of the ASU User Class, then a message will be included in the Patient Cohort section indicating that the ASU User Class was not found.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The new Polytrauma Assessment reminder dialog was created outside the OI Field Office and tested at three sites based on input from Rehabilitation Service stakeholders.
texts.text: The new Polytrauma Assessment reminder dialog was created outside the OI Field Office and tested at three sites based on input from Rehabilitation Service stakeholders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This reminder/dialog includes branching logic that will use the new national reminder term that is defined with the ASU user class used by the reminder definition. The branching logic will check to see if the CPRS user is a member of the ASU user class before continuing with the reminder dialog.  If the CPRS user is not a member of the ASU user class, then the reminder dialog will display text indicating the reminder is not applicable and the user is not the appropriate user to complete the reminder dialog.
texts.text: This reminder/dialog includes branching logic that will use the new national reminder term that is defined with the ASU user class used by the reminder definition. The branching logic will check to see if the CPRS user is a member of the ASU user class before continuing with the reminder dialog.  If the CPRS user is not a member of the ASU user class, then the reminder dialog will display text indicating the reminder is not applicable and the user is not the appropriate user to complete the reminder dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Completing the reminder dialog will cause a progress note to be created and Health Factors will be populated in PCE. These health factors will be used by future reminder definition evaluation to indicate the reminder is resolved.
texts.text: Completing the reminder dialog will cause a progress note to be created and Health Factors will be populated in PCE. These health factors will be used by future reminder definition evaluation to indicate the reminder is resolved.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE: This project raised a question about which sites use the PXRM NEW PARAMETER mechanism to determine which reminders should be presented to a user. In order for developers to answer this question, a new mechanism is needed at the sites to send information to the developers in a MailMan message.
texts.text: NOTE: This project raised a question about which sites use the PXRM NEW PARAMETER mechanism to determine which reminders should be presented to a user. In order for developers to answer this question, a new mechanism is needed at the sites to send information to the developers in a MailMan message.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Upon installation of the PXRM*2*17 patch, the post-installation process will check the local system’s setting of the PXRM NEW PARAMETER setting and will send a MailMan message to a FORUM Mail Group that will be defined with reminder developers as members.  The MailMan message will contain the site and its current PXRM NEW PARAMETER setting.
texts.text: Upon installation of the PXRM*2*17 patch, the post-installation process will check the local system’s setting of the PXRM NEW PARAMETER setting and will send a MailMan message to a FORUM Mail Group that will be defined with reminder developers as members.  The MailMan message will contain the site and its current PXRM NEW PARAMETER setting.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If your site doesn’t have NEW REMINDERS PARAMETER set to yes, then the Reminder Manager will not be able to assign the polytrauma reminder by user class.
texts.text: If your site doesn’t have NEW REMINDERS PARAMETER set to yes, then the Reminder Manager will not be able to assign the polytrauma reminder by user class.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Polytrauma Marker project releases a new national reminder and reminder dialog that Polytrauma Specialty providers can use to identify potential OEF/OIF Polytrauma patients and mark with a Polytrauma Marker (health factor), when appropriate.
texts.text: The Polytrauma Marker project releases a new national reminder and reminder dialog that Polytrauma Specialty providers can use to identify potential OEF/OIF Polytrauma patients and mark with a Polytrauma Marker (health factor), when appropriate.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Polytrauma Marker project includes two patches:
texts.text: The Polytrauma Marker project includes two patches:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PXRM*2*17 -	Clinical Reminder patch with Polytrauma Marker reminder/dialog
texts.text: PXRM*2*17 -	Clinical Reminder patch with Polytrauma Marker reminder/dialog

texts.label: text
texts.orig: USR*1*33 -	ASU patch with API that checks to see if a user is a member of a specific User Class
texts.text: USR*1*33 -	ASU patch with API that checks to see if a user is a member of a specific User Class

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch changes displays of User Class Names in Authorization and   Subscription Utility (ASU) options. User Class Names are now shown by the name of the entry in the User Class File (File #8930).  Previously, they were shown by their display names. In particular, the User Class Definition Option [USR CLASS DEFINITION] has been changed to show the entry name of the User Class.
texts.text: This patch changes displays of User Class Names in Authorization and   Subscription Utility (ASU) options. User Class Names are now shown by the name of the entry in the User Class File (File #8930).  Previously, they were shown by their display names. In particular, the User Class Definition Option [USR CLASS DEFINITION] has been changed to show the entry name of the User Class.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The User Class Definition Option [USR CLASS DEFINITION] is found under the User Class Management Menu [USR CLASS MANAGEMENT MENU], which is found under the TIU Maintenance Menu [TIU IRM MAINTENANCE MENU].
texts.text: The User Class Definition Option [USR CLASS DEFINITION] is found under the User Class Management Menu [USR CLASS MANAGEMENT MENU], which is found under the TIU Maintenance Menu [TIU IRM MAINTENANCE MENU].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition, the module used to list the User Classes a particular user belongs to has been enhanced. It may now be set to list classes by entry name rather than by display name, if desired.
texts.text: In addition, the module used to list the User Classes a particular user belongs to has been enhanced. It may now be set to list classes by entry name rather than by display name, if desired.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These changes are necessary because it is the entry name of the Class that users see in User Class edits and various ASU modules. Using the same name in displays avoids confusion.
texts.text: These changes are necessary because it is the entry name of the Class that users see in User Class edits and various ASU modules. Using the same name in displays avoids confusion.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following example is from a medical center that has populated its User Class File (#8930) with User Class Names in upper case (as in ACCOUNTANT) and Display Names with title capitalization (as in Accountant). Thus in the User Class Definition Option [USR CLASS DEFINITION] all names that appear in the list of active user classes are the actual User Class Names and thus are all in upper case:
texts.text: The following example is from a medical center that has populated its User Class File (#8930) with User Class Names in upper case (as in ACCOUNTANT) and Display Names with title capitalization (as in Accountant). Thus in the User Class Definition Option [USR CLASS DEFINITION] all names that appear in the list of active user classes are the actual User Class Names and thus are all in upper case:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is the User Class Name, not the Display Name.
texts.text: This is the User Class Name, not the Display Name.

texts.text: 14   ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER OF THE DAY                   AOD

texts.text: Create a Class            List Members              Quit

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Three national computed findings are included:
texts.text: Three national computed findings are included:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This multi-occurrence computed finding returns a list of the ASU User Classes that the user belongs to. The user is the person who is running the reminder evaluation or processing a reminder dialog.
texts.text: This multi-occurrence computed finding returns a list of the ASU User Classes that the user belongs to. The user is the person who is running the reminder evaluation or processing a reminder dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a user is assigned to an ASU User Class, there can be an Activation Date and an Expiration Date. For the purpose of this computed finding, the date of the finding will be the Activation Date. In those cases when there is no Activation Date, 00/00/0000 will be displayed as the date of the finding. If a Beginning Date/Time and Ending Date/Time are used with the finding, then the period defined by the Activation Date and Expiration Date must overlap the period defined by the Beginning Date/Time and the Ending Date/Time, in order for the finding to be true. When the Activation Date is missing, a 0 will be used in its place for determining the overlap. When the Expiration Date is missing, the evaluation date will be used in its place for determining the overlap.
texts.text: When a user is assigned to an ASU User Class, there can be an Activation Date and an Expiration Date. For the purpose of this computed finding, the date of the finding will be the Activation Date. In those cases when there is no Activation Date, 00/00/0000 will be displayed as the date of the finding. If a Beginning Date/Time and Ending Date/Time are used with the finding, then the period defined by the Activation Date and Expiration Date must overlap the period defined by the Beginning Date/Time and the Ending Date/Time, in order for the finding to be true. When the Activation Date is missing, a 0 will be used in its place for determining the overlap. When the Expiration Date is missing, the evaluation date will be used in its place for determining the overlap.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Computed Finding Parameter can be used to specify either the internal entry number or the exact name of an ASU User Class. If this option is used, then each User Class the user is a member of is checked to see if it is either the specified User Class or a child of the specified User Class. Only the User Classes that pass this test will remain on the list of the user's User Classes. For example, if the user is a member of the user class ATTENDING PHYSICIAN, which is a child of the user class PHYSICIAN, and the Computed Finding Parameter is set to "PHYSICIAN," then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN will remain on the list. If the Computed Finding Parameter was set to CARDIOLOGIST, then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN would be removed since it is not a child of CARDIOLOGIST.
texts.text: The Computed Finding Parameter can be used to specify either the internal entry number or the exact name of an ASU User Class. If this option is used, then each User Class the user is a member of is checked to see if it is either the specified User Class or a child of the specified User Class. Only the User Classes that pass this test will remain on the list of the user's User Classes. For example, if the user is a member of the user class ATTENDING PHYSICIAN, which is a child of the user class PHYSICIAN, and the Computed Finding Parameter is set to "PHYSICIAN," then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN will remain on the list. If the Computed Finding Parameter was set to CARDIOLOGIST, then ATTENDING PHYSICIAN would be removed since it is not a child of CARDIOLOGIST.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you want to specify the user class as PHYSICIAN, type the following in the computed finding parameter field: COMPUTED FINDING PARAMETER:PHYSICIAN
texts.text: If you want to specify the user class as PHYSICIAN, type the following in the computed finding parameter field: COMPUTED FINDING PARAMETER:PHYSICIAN

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This computed finding uses date ranges like drug findings do, so if the user was an active member of the class anytime during the date range, the computed finding will be true. If you want to know if the user is active as of today, then use T for the beginning and ending date/time.
texts.text: This computed finding uses date ranges like drug findings do, so if the user was an active member of the class anytime during the date range, the computed finding will be true. If you want to know if the user is active as of today, then use T for the beginning and ending date/time.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This multi-occurrence computed finding will search the Patient Movement File for a list of patient admissions and associated discharges. If the date range defined by the admission date and discharge date overlap the date range defined by the Beginning Date/Time and Ending Date/Time, the admission discharge pair will be kept on the list. If the patient's last admission does not have an associated discharge, then the evaluation date will be used in place of the discharge date in the overlap calculation.
texts.text: This multi-occurrence computed finding will search the Patient Movement File for a list of patient admissions and associated discharges. If the date range defined by the admission date and discharge date overlap the date range defined by the Beginning Date/Time and Ending Date/Time, the admission discharge pair will be kept on the list. If the patient's last admission does not have an associated discharge, then the evaluation date will be used in place of the discharge date in the overlap calculation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The date of the finding will be the admission date, unless the Computed Finding Parameter is set to "DISCH," in which case the discharge date will be used. If you want to use discharge date as date of finding in the computed finding parameter field, type the following:  COMPUTED FINDING PARAMETER:DISCH
texts.text: The date of the finding will be the admission date, unless the Computed Finding Parameter is set to "DISCH," in which case the discharge date will be used. If you want to use discharge date as date of finding in the computed finding parameter field, type the following:  COMPUTED FINDING PARAMETER:DISCH

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The "CSUB" values returned by this computed finding are:
texts.text: The "CSUB" values returned by this computed finding are:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This multiple occurrence computed finding will return active patient record flags. The Computed Finding Parameter is used to precisely specify what flags to search for. Three parameters can be used: C for category, T for type, and F for a specific flag.
texts.text: This multiple occurrence computed finding will return active patient record flags. The Computed Finding Parameter is used to precisely specify what flags to search for. Three parameters can be used: C for category, T for type, and F for a specific flag.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Type - B (behavioral), C (clinical), O (other), and R (research)
texts.text: Type - B (behavioral), C (clinical), O (other), and R (research)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Flag - exact name of the flag
texts.text: Flag - exact name of the flag

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Use the "^" character to include more than one parameter in the search specification.
texts.text: Use the "^" character to include more than one parameter in the search specification.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: C:N^T:B - searches for national flags whose type is behavioral
texts.text: C:N^T:B - searches for national flags whose type is behavioral

texts.label: text
texts.orig: F:DRINKING PROBLEM - searches for the flag DRINKING PROBLEM
texts.text: F:DRINKING PROBLEM - searches for the flag DRINKING PROBLEM

texts.label: text
texts.orig: T:C^F:INFECTIOUS DISEASE - searches for the flag INFECTIOUS DISEASE whose type is clinical
texts.text: T:C^F:INFECTIOUS DISEASE - searches for the flag INFECTIOUS DISEASE whose type is clinical

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Only active flags that meet all the specified criteria will be returned. If no search parameters are specified, then no flags will be returned.
texts.text: Only active flags that meet all the specified criteria will be returned. If no search parameters are specified, then no flags will be returned.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The date associated with a flag is the assigned date.
texts.text: The date associated with a flag is the assigned date.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following "CSUB" data is returned for each flag that is found:
texts.text: The following "CSUB" data is returned for each flag that is found:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TIULINK  - pointer to the TIU note (only applies if the flag is linked to a
texts.text: TIULINK  - pointer to the TIU note (only applies if the flag is linked to a

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TIUTITLE - the note title (only applies if the flag is linked to a
texts.text: TIUTITLE - the note title (only applies if the flag is linked to a

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The resolution date calculation was not correct when the resolution logic contained a “NOT”. In those cases where the resolution logic evaluated to true, the resolution date was calculated to be 0. This was corrected so that the resolution date will no longer be calculated as 0.
texts.text: The resolution date calculation was not correct when the resolution logic contained a “NOT”. In those cases where the resolution logic evaluated to true, the resolution date was calculated to be 0. This was corrected so that the resolution date will no longer be calculated as 0.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Clinical Maintenance output was not displaying the resolution finding header when the resolution logic evaluated to false, but one or more of the resolution findings was true. This was corrected.
texts.text: The Clinical Maintenance output was not displaying the resolution finding header when the resolution logic evaluated to false, but one or more of the resolution findings was true. This was corrected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There was a bug in the display of drug findings; for multiple drug findings in a term, only one occurrence of each finding type was displaying. This was corrected.
texts.text: There was a bug in the display of drug findings; for multiple drug findings in a term, only one occurrence of each finding type was displaying. This was corrected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Other problems were reported with the display of drug findings. A complete fix of this pre-existing problem will require more time and testing than we have available for this patch, so a complete solution will have to come in a future patch. Nevertheless, one problem was rectified: The output was not consistent across inpatient, outpatient, and non-VA meds. The output was changed so that it is as consistent as possible (there are some fields that apply only to outpatient and some that apply only to non-VA.)
texts.text: Other problems were reported with the display of drug findings. A complete fix of this pre-existing problem will require more time and testing than we have available for this patch, so a complete solution will have to come in a future patch. Nevertheless, one problem was rectified: The output was not consistent across inpatient, outpatient, and non-VA meds. The output was changed so that it is as consistent as possible (there are some fields that apply only to outpatient and some that apply only to non-VA.)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A bug was reported where a non-CH lab test would not work when used as a term finding, but would work when used as a definition finding. This is corrected.
texts.text: A bug was reported where a non-CH lab test would not work when used as a term finding, but would work when used as a definition finding. This is corrected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A problem with an incorrect resolution date calculation for a resolution logic string containing a 'FF was corrected.
texts.text: A problem with an incorrect resolution date calculation for a resolution logic string containing a 'FF was corrected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patch PXRM*2*12 added the new field OWNER to reminder report templates. E3R 20277 requested including this field when displaying the details of the template. This functionality has been added. If an owner is defined, the template will list the owner name. If an owner is not defined, the template will display "None" next to the owner field.
texts.text: Patch PXRM*2*12 added the new field OWNER to reminder report templates. E3R 20277 requested including this field when displaying the details of the template. This functionality has been added. If an owner is defined, the template will list the owner name. If an owner is not defined, the template will display "None" next to the owner field.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Remedy ticket 372679 reported a bug where the Reminders Due Report was not producing any output when the selected output device was a host file. This was found to be the case at some, but not all sites. The reason for this is that sites can name a host file device anything they want and the report was expecting a certain set of names. The solution was to use the type instead of the name, because types are standardized.
texts.text: Remedy ticket 372679 reported a bug where the Reminders Due Report was not producing any output when the selected output device was a host file. This was found to be the case at some, but not all sites. The reason for this is that sites can name a host file device anything they want and the report was expecting a certain set of names. The solution was to use the type instead of the name, because types are standardized.

texts.text: Mental Health and Behavioral Science Strategic Group

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A new option, PXRM TAXONOMY CODE SEARCH, has been added to the TAXONOMY MANAGEMENT MENU. This option will let the user input a code and then it will search all taxonomies in the account and list those where it is used.
texts.text: A new option, PXRM TAXONOMY CODE SEARCH, has been added to the TAXONOMY MANAGEMENT MENU. This option will let the user input a code and then it will search all taxonomies in the account and list those where it is used.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The mechanism used by the Clinical Reminders package to deliver MailMan messages was changed to make it more flexible.
texts.text: The mechanism used by the Clinical Reminders package to deliver MailMan messages was changed to make it more flexible.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When PXRM*2.0*17 is installed in a test account, a MailMan message containing the value of ORQQPX NEW REMINDER PARAMS will be sent to the Clinical Reminders mail group. When the installation is done in a production account, the message will be sent to a Forum mail group. If a site doesn’t have NEW REMINDERS PARAMETER set to yes, then the Reminders Manager will not be able to assign the Polytrauma reminder by user class and that is the reason for the new computed finding. So if a site has a problem and Reminders developers or customer support staff know that a site has the parameter set to NO, developers or support staff can tell the site how to fix it.
texts.text: When PXRM*2.0*17 is installed in a test account, a MailMan message containing the value of ORQQPX NEW REMINDER PARAMS will be sent to the Clinical Reminders mail group. When the installation is done in a production account, the message will be sent to a Forum mail group. If a site doesn’t have NEW REMINDERS PARAMETER set to yes, then the Reminders Manager will not be able to assign the Polytrauma reminder by user class and that is the reason for the new computed finding. So if a site has a problem and Reminders developers or customer support staff know that a site has the parameter set to NO, developers or support staff can tell the site how to fix it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A problem with the MST History file not updating after recording one of the MST health factors was reported; Remedy ticket #367613. A fix for this problem is included and the post-init will start an MST synchronization job to ensure that the MST History file is up-to-date.
texts.text: A problem with the MST History file not updating after recording one of the MST health factors was reported; Remedy ticket #367613. A fix for this problem is included and the post-init will start an MST synchronization job to ensure that the MST History file is up-to-date.

texts.text: A problem with an incorrect resolution date calculation for a resolution logic string containing a 'FF was corrected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prior to patch PXRM*2*17, there was a conflict between the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN reminder definition and its dialog. On the coversheet, the reminder was showing as DUE NOW, but when the dialog was opened, there were no items to complete. Briefly, this was because the two health factors DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG and DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS were used in the definition with an ending date/time of 11/30/06 while in the dialog they had an ending date/time of 9/30/08. To resolve the problem, the Ending Date/Time was removed from the health factors in the definition. Complete details can be found below. If you are not interested, please skip the next section.
texts.text: Prior to patch PXRM*2*17, there was a conflict between the VA-IRAQ & AFGHAN POST-DEPLOY SCREEN reminder definition and its dialog. On the coversheet, the reminder was showing as DUE NOW, but when the dialog was opened, there were no items to complete. Briefly, this was because the two health factors DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG and DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS were used in the definition with an ending date/time of 11/30/06 while in the dialog they had an ending date/time of 9/30/08. To resolve the problem, the Ending Date/Time was removed from the health factors in the definition. Complete details can be found below. If you are not interested, please skip the next section.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Background for the date conflict: One of the findings in the reminder definition was the reminder term VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN OEF/OIF, and two of its mapped findings were the health factors DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG and DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS. Both of these mapped findings had an ending date/time of 11/30/06. However, the dialog used the branching term VA-BL DEPRESSION SCREENING that was mapped to the computed finding VA-REMINDER DEFINITION. VA-REMINDER DEFINITION evaluated the reminder definition VA-BL DEPRESSION SCREEN with the Condition checking for a status of “DUE NOW”.  Two of the findings in this reminder definition were the terms: VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN NEGATIVE, containing the health factor DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG as a mapped finding, and VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN POSITIVE, containing the health factor DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS—both with an Ending Date/Time of 9/30/08. As noted above, the same two health factors were used in the term VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN OEF/OIF, but they had an Ending Date/Time of 11/30/06. Therefore, if a patient had either of these health factors given between the dates of 11/30/06 and 9/30/08, we would see this inconsistency. To resolve this problem, the Ending Date/Time on these health factors in the term VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN OEF/OIF was removed, but they were left in the terms VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN NEGATIVE and VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN POSITIVE. The reason was that this requires sites to do less remapping. After this change, if a patient has a health factor of DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG or DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS given between the dates of 11/30/06 and 9/30/08, the reminder will no longer show as due on the coversheet. This change is made when the Reminder Exchange entry DEPRESSION/PTSD REMINDER TERM UPDATES - PATCH 17 is installed during the post-init.
texts.text: Background for the date conflict: One of the findings in the reminder definition was the reminder term VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN OEF/OIF, and two of its mapped findings were the health factors DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG and DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS. Both of these mapped findings had an ending date/time of 11/30/06. However, the dialog used the branching term VA-BL DEPRESSION SCREENING that was mapped to the computed finding VA-REMINDER DEFINITION. VA-REMINDER DEFINITION evaluated the reminder definition VA-BL DEPRESSION SCREEN with the Condition checking for a status of “DUE NOW”.  Two of the findings in this reminder definition were the terms: VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN NEGATIVE, containing the health factor DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG as a mapped finding, and VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN POSITIVE, containing the health factor DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS—both with an Ending Date/Time of 9/30/08. As noted above, the same two health factors were used in the term VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN OEF/OIF, but they had an Ending Date/Time of 11/30/06. Therefore, if a patient had either of these health factors given between the dates of 11/30/06 and 9/30/08, we would see this inconsistency. To resolve this problem, the Ending Date/Time on these health factors in the term VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN OEF/OIF was removed, but they were left in the terms VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN NEGATIVE and VA-DEPRESSION SCREEN POSITIVE. The reason was that this requires sites to do less remapping. After this change, if a patient has a health factor of DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION NEG or DEP SCREEN 2 QUESTION POS given between the dates of 11/30/06 and 9/30/08, the reminder will no longer show as due on the coversheet. This change is made when the Reminder Exchange entry DEPRESSION/PTSD REMINDER TERM UPDATES - PATCH 17 is installed during the post-init.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Given that some sites have had problems with MHA, the MH instruments cannot be required at all sites and health factors may still be in use at some sites as late as FY10.  These old screening tools need to be included as valid.
texts.text: Given that some sites have had problems with MHA, the MH instruments cannot be required at all sites and health factors may still be in use at some sites as late as FY10.  These old screening tools need to be included as valid.

texts.orig: Problems installing TIU Health Summary Objects
texts.text: Problems installing TIU Health Summary Objects

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When installing a health summary type via Reminder Exchange, if any of the selection items are missing, users will now be able to delete the selection item, replace it with another one, or delete it from the health summary type.
texts.text: When installing a health summary type via Reminder Exchange, if any of the selection items are missing, users will now be able to delete the selection item, replace it with another one, or delete it from the health summary type.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the past, if you tried to install a TIU Health Summary Object without installing the prerequisites first, the object method would not have the correct form, making it non-functional (even though it seemed as if it installed properly). Reminder Exchange now checks to make sure the Health Summary Object name can be resolved into an internal entry number so it will function properly. If it cannot be resolved, an error message is displayed telling the user to install the prerequisites and then it returns the user back to the main Exchange screen.
texts.text: In the past, if you tried to install a TIU Health Summary Object without installing the prerequisites first, the object method would not have the correct form, making it non-functional (even though it seemed as if it installed properly). Reminder Exchange now checks to make sure the Health Summary Object name can be resolved into an internal entry number so it will function properly. If it cannot be resolved, an error message is displayed telling the user to install the prerequisites and then it returns the user back to the main Exchange screen.
